([simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 庐江县; [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 廬江縣; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Lújiāng Xiàn*) is a [county](/wiki/Counties_of_China "Counties of China") of [Anhui Province](/wiki/Anhui_Province "Anhui Province") in [East China](/wiki/East_China "East China"). It is under the administration of the [prefecture-level city](/wiki/Prefecture-level_city "Prefecture-level city") of [Hefei](/wiki/Hefei "Hefei"), the capital of Anhui. It is the southernmost [county-level division](/wiki/County-level_division "County-level division") of Hefei. Lujiang has a population of 888,238 as of November 2020[[1]](#cite_note-2020CensusHefei-1) and covers an area of 2,343.7 km2 (904.9 sq mi). The county government is located in Lucheng Town.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

"Lujiang" redirects here. For the Guangzhou Metro station, see [Lujiang Station](/wiki/Lujiang_Station "Lujiang Station").

County in Anhui, China

**Lujiang County** ([simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 庐江县; [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 廬江縣; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Lújiāng Xiàn*) is a [county](/wiki/Counties_of_China "Counties of China") of [Anhui Province](/wiki/Anhui_Province "Anhui Province") in [East China](/wiki/East_China "East China"). It is under the administration of the [prefecture-level city](/wiki/Prefecture-level_city "Prefecture-level city") of [Hefei](/wiki/Hefei "Hefei"), the capital of Anhui. It is the southernmost [county-level division](/wiki/County-level_division "County-level division") of Hefei. Lujiang has a population of 888,238 as of November 2020[[1]](#cite_note-2020CensusHefei-1) and covers an area of 2,343.7 km2 (904.9 sq mi). The county government is located in Lucheng Town.

Lujiang County has jurisdiction over 17 towns.

During the [Spring and Autumn period](/wiki/Spring_and_Autumn_period "Spring and Autumn period"), the Lujiang area was the location of the minor [State of Shu (舒)](/w/index.php?title=State_of_Shu_(%E8%88%92)&action=edit&redlink=1 "State of Shu (舒) (page does not exist)") [[zh](https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%88%92%E5%9B%BD "zh:舒国")].

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lujiang_County&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Lujiang County used to be administered by the former prefecture-level city of Chaohu until August 2011, when Chaohu was dissolved and Lujiang, along with the former Juchao District (now the county-level [Chaohu City](/wiki/Chaohu "Chaohu")), was incorporated into Hefei.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Administrative divisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lujiang_County&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Administrative divisions")]

Lujiang County is divided into 17 towns.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

* Lucheng (庐城镇)
* Yefushan (冶父山镇)
* Tangchi (汤池镇)
* Wanshan (万山镇)
* Jinniu (金牛镇)
* Leqiao (乐桥镇)
* Guohe (郭河镇)
* Shitou (石头镇)
* Baishan (白山镇)
* Tongda (同大镇)
* Ketan (柯坦镇)
* Shengqiao (盛桥镇)
* Longqiao (龙桥镇)
* Baihu (白湖镇)
* Fanshan (矾山镇)
* Nihe (泥河镇)
* Luohe (罗河镇)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lujiang_County&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Economy")]

Lujiang County is home to the Shaxi Copper Mine, operated by [Tongling Nonferrous Metals Group](/wiki/Tongling_Nonferrous_Metals_Group "Tongling Nonferrous Metals Group"). Although primarily a copper mine, it has become the largest gold-producing mine in China. In 2022, it produced approximately 958,740 ounces of gold, making it the leading single source of domestic gold output.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
